---
title: "EDA 61 — Exposure Variable Profile: 17 Chronic Condition Flags"
subtitle: "Andersen Behavioral Model — Primary Exposure Domain"
author: "Analysis Team"
date: "2026-05-22"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    code-fold: true
    theme: yeti
    highlight-style: breeze
    code-line-numbers: true
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    keep-md: false
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Mission

This EDA profiles the 17 chronic-condition exposure variables (`cc_*`) that constitute
the primary predictor domain in the Andersen Behavioral Model framework. It directly
addresses §2.2 (primary exposure definition) and previews §4.3 (bivariate associations)
from the statistical instructions.

**Research Questions:**

1. What is the weighted prevalence of each chronic condition in the employed population?
2. How does each chronic condition relate to the primary outcome (`days_absent_total`) —
   mean days, median days, and zero-proportion?
3. What is the missing-data pattern across the 17 `cc_*` variables?
4. What is the multimorbidity structure — how many conditions do respondents report,
   and which condition pairs most frequently co-occur?

Following the dual-file development pattern, all analytical code lives in
`analysis/eda-61/eda-61.R`. This document sources those chunks for presentation.

# Definition of Terms

**Chronic condition (`cc_*`)**: A long-term health condition (expected duration ≥6 months)
diagnosed by a health professional. Coded as R logical (`TRUE`/`FALSE`). Source: CCHS
Canadian Community Health Survey chronic conditions module (CCC).

**Multimorbidity**: The co-occurrence of two or more chronic conditions in the same
individual.

**`days_absent_total`**: Primary outcome. Row-wise sum of all 8 LOP component variables,
representing total workdays missed in the past 3 months due to health reasons.

**Exposure domain**: In the Andersen Behavioral Model, chronic conditions represent the
mechanism through which predisposing, facilitating, and needs factors translate into
absenteeism.

```{r}
#| code-summary: Reconciles working directory between execution modes
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| warning: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
library(knitr)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "../../")
```

```{r}
#| label: set_options
#| code-summary: Report-wide formatting options
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
report_render_start_time <- Sys.time()
opts_chunk$set(
  results   = "show",
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  comment   = NA,
  tidy      = FALSE,
  out.width = "960px",
  fig.width  = 10,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.path   = "figure-png-iso/"
)
options(width = 100)
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal())
read_chunk("analysis/eda-61/eda-61.R")
```

# Environment

> Reviews the components of the working environment. Non-technical readers may skip this section.

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| code-summary: Packages used in the current report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: load-sources
#| code-summary: Collection of custom functions used in current repository
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: declare-globals
#| code-summary: Values used throughout the report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: declare-functions
#| code-summary: Custom functions defined for use in this report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

# Data

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| code-summary: Load data from Ellis parquet outputs
#| warning: false
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

## Data Context

For complete data documentation, see the
[Data Primer](../data-primer-1/data-primer-1.html).

```{r}
#| label: data-context-tables
#| code-summary: Tables and variables used in this analysis
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: data-context-person
#| code-summary: Representative individual with multiple chronic conditions
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: data-context-distributions
#| code-summary: Prevalence summary of 17 chronic condition variables
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: tweak-data-0
#| code-summary: Reshape to long-form for analysis
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: inspect-data-0
#| code-summary: Data overview
#| code-fold: true
```

# Analysis

## Graph Family G1: Condition Prevalence

<!-- Research question: What is the weighted prevalence of each chronic condition? -->

```{r}
#| label: g1-data-prep
#| code-summary: Prepare prevalence summaries
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g1
#| code-summary: Unweighted prevalence of chronic conditions
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Unweighted prevalence of 17 chronic conditions in the analytical sample"
```

```{r}
#| label: g11
#| code-summary: Weighted prevalence of chronic conditions
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Population-weighted prevalence using wts_m_pooled"
```

```{r}
#| label: g12
#| code-summary: Weighted prevalence by CCHS cycle
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Condition prevalence comparison between 2010-2011 and 2013-2014 cycles"
```

## Graph Family G2: Outcome Relationship

<!-- Research question: How does each condition relate to days_absent_total? -->

```{r}
#| label: g2-data-prep
#| code-summary: Prepare outcome summaries by condition
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g2
#| code-summary: Mean days absent by condition (those with condition)
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Weighted mean days absent among those reporting each condition"
```

```{r}
#| label: g21
#| code-summary: Median days absent (IQR) by condition
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Median and interquartile range of days absent for each condition"
```

```{r}
#| label: g22
#| code-summary: Zero-day proportion by condition status
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Proportion reporting zero days absent, contrasting condition present vs absent"
```

## Graph Family G3: Missing Data Profile

<!-- Research question: What is the missing-data pattern across 17 cc_* variables? -->

```{r}
#| label: g3-data-prep
#| code-summary: Compute missingness statistics
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g3
#| code-summary: Missingness profile across chronic condition variables
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Percentage and count of missing values per condition flag"
```

```{r}
#| label: g31
#| code-summary: Missingness by CCHS cycle
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Missing data rates by survey cycle"
```

## Graph Family G4: Multimorbidity / Co-occurrence

<!-- Research question: What is the multimorbidity structure? -->

```{r}
#| label: g4-data-prep
#| code-summary: Compute condition counts and co-occurrence matrix
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g4
#| code-summary: Distribution of condition count per respondent
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Weighted distribution of number of chronic conditions per respondent"
```

```{r}
#| label: g41
#| code-summary: Top 20 co-occurring condition pairs (dot plot)
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Top 20 condition pairs ranked by co-occurrence rate"
```
